today.title: Today
today.attention.heading: Attention
today.activity.heading: Since last check
today.homework.dueDateInferredTitle: Due date not posted — estimated to be the next school day
classes.title: Classes
classes.empty: No grade data yet. Tap Refresh to check.
history.title: History
history.empty.noUrl: To track homework for this child, add a Google Sites URL under Settings → Children.
history.empty.noDataAtAll: No homework recorded yet for this child.
common.welcomeBody: No children are set up yet. Add your first child and we'll keep track of grades and missing assignments — right here on your computer.
common.readQuickStart: New here? Read the quick start →
settings.children.title: Children
settings.children.help: The kids this app tracks. Each row edits name, TeacherEase login, and optional homework URL.
settings.children.addHelp: Validates the TeacherEase login before saving so a bad password doesn't silently land. Homework URL is optional.
settings.children.credentialsNote: Credentials stored securely on this computer
settings.children.field.name: Child's name
settings.children.placeholder.name: e.g. Alex
settings.children.placeholder.passwordKe: Leave blank to keep current password
settings.children.confirmRemove.title: Remove {name}?
settings.children.confirmRemove.body: Deletes all local grade and homework history. TeacherEase is not affected.
settings.appearance.language.title: Language
settings.appearance.language.help: Pick the app's display language. System follows your operating system's language; otherwise the app stays in the language you choose.
settings.appearance.language.label: Language
settings.appearance.profile.title: Profile
settings.appearance.profile.help: Five curated palettes with light + dark variants. Pick the one that matches your eye; descriptions below preview each.
settings.appearance.profile.default: Warm off-white + warm slate. The app's house palette.
settings.appearance.profile.solarized: Ethan Schoonover's classic warm-ochre and cyan.
settings.appearance.profile.nord: Cool Arctic blue-gray. Calm and modern.
settings.appearance.profile.dracula: Purple / pink / cyan on dark. Dark-first classic.
settings.appearance.profile.contrast: WCAG AAA. Maximum contrast for accessibility.
settings.appearance.mode.title: Mode
settings.appearance.mode.help: System follows your operating system's light/dark preference live. Light and Dark lock the app to that mode regardless of the OS.
settings.appearance.size.title: Size
settings.appearance.size.help: Scales everything — text, icons, spacing, borders — proportionally. Pick a preset or type a custom percentage.
settings.attention.forgiveness.title: Forgiveness window
settings.attention.forgiveness.help: How long a missing or low-score item keeps demanding attention before quietly aging into Older. These knobs don't touch TeacherEase grades — they shape the ! / ✓ layer this app overlays. Default 2 weeks.
settings.attention.threshold.title: Low-score threshold
settings.attention.threshold.help: Scores strictly below this count as attention-worthy. TeacherEase caps at 3 (Meeting); default 3.0 flags anything below Meeting. Lower to 2.0 if you only want clearly-below-Progressing. These knobs don't touch TeacherEase grades.
settings.attention.icons.title: Icon reference
settings.attention.icons.help: What the icons in the Classes drilldown mean.
settings.attention.icons.missingRecent.d: Assignment flagged as not turned in, within the forgiveness window.
settings.attention.icons.missingOlder.de: Missing item past the forgiveness window — no longer demanding action.
settings.attention.icons.lowRecent.label: Low score (recent)
settings.attention.icons.lowRecent.desc: Graded below the low-score threshold, within the forgiveness window.
settings.attention.icons.lowOlder.label: Low score (older)
settings.attention.icons.lowOlder.desc: Low-score item past the forgiveness window.
settings.attention.icons.meeting.label: Meeting
settings.attention.icons.meeting.desc: Graded at or above the threshold — no attention needed.
settings.attention.icons.notGraded.label: Not graded
settings.attention.icons.notGraded.desc: Posted to the gradebook but no score entered yet (upcoming or pending).
settings.fetch.schedule.title: Schedule
settings.fetch.schedule.help: How often — and anchored where — the app pulls fresh data from the portal. Slots are evenly spaced from “First slot at” around the 24h cycle. Only fires while the app is open; autostart keeps it ticking.
settings.fetch.now.title: Fetch now
settings.fetch.now.help: Pulls the latest data for every child immediately. No notification fires — only the database updates. Use this when you want to see current data without waiting for the next scheduled run.
settings.fetch.lastSuccess.title: Last successful fetch
settings.fetch.lastSuccess.help: When each child was last pulled via the TeacherEase source. Empty when a child has never successfully scraped.
settings.notifications.desktop.title: Desktop
settings.notifications.desktop.help: A hero-level system notification at each scheduled slot — what needs attention, today's homework counts.
settings.notifications.desktop.osDigestD: Toggle system notifications on or off.
settings.notifications.email.title: Email
settings.notifications.email.help: SMTP-based daily digest with per-child detail. You bring the SMTP credentials; nothing relays through our servers. Click Update to change config and fire a test email.
settings.notifications.schedule.title: Schedule
settings.notifications.schedule.help: How many times per day — and anchored where — a digest fires. By default each run pulls grades and homework from TeacherEase right before sending so the digest reflects current portal state; turn off 'Fetch fresh data first' to skip the pull and use whatever data is already in the database (faster, but may be stale).
settings.notifications.send.title: Send digest now
settings.notifications.send.help: Builds a digest from current data and dispatches through your enabled channels (respects the toggles above). Useful to preview what the next scheduled notification will look like.
settings.notifications.send.dispatched: Dispatched via your enabled channels — check them now.
settings.email.summary.detailConfigured: SMTP server + recipients. Click Update to change.
settings.email.summary.detailNotConfigur: Send the daily digest to one or more inboxes.
settings.email.digest.title: Email digest
settings.email.digest.descConfigured: Send the daily digest through the SMTP server above.
settings.email.digest.descNotConfigured: Set up SMTP above to enable.
settings.email.form.heading: SMTP server
settings.email.form.byo: BYO SMTP — we never relay through a server. Password stays on this computer, in the app's local database.
settings.email.form.gmailHint: For Gmail, use an App Password (2-step verification required).
settings.advanced.updates.title: Updates
settings.advanced.updates.help: Installed version. The app quietly checks GitHub Releases at most once every 24 hours when you open this tab. When an update is available, clicking Install downloads + verifies the signature + replaces the app + relaunches automatically. First-install users get installers from GitHub Releases; after that, updates flow here.
settings.advanced.general.title: General
settings.advanced.general.startOnLoginDe: Launch the app automatically when you sign in.
settings.advanced.danger.title: Danger zone
settings.advanced.danger.help: One-way deletion. Back up your data if in doubt.
settings.advanced.danger.confirmTitle: Reset the app to first-install state?
settings.advanced.danger.confirmBody: Wipes all local data. Cannot be undone. The app will quit.
settings.advanced.danger.idleBody: Wipes all app data and returns to first-install state.
wizard.subtitle: Please read and acknowledge before continuing.
wizard.disclaimer.heading: Disclaimer
wizard.disclaimer.full:
This is an unofficial, community-built tool. It is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to TeacherEase, Common Goal Systems Inc., or any school district.

This software accesses TeacherEase using credentials that you provide for your own parent account. It exercises the same access you have when logging in through a web browser — no additional permissions, no security bypasses, no access to other users' data.

By using this software, you acknowledge that:

• You are the authorized parent/guardian for the TeacherEase account(s) you configure.
• You accept responsibility for your use of this tool in accordance with your school's policies and TeacherEase's Terms of Service.
• The developer provides this software "as is" with no warranty of any kind.
• The developer is not responsible for any consequences of using this software, including but not limited to account restrictions that may be imposed by TeacherEase or your school.
wizard.disclaimer.privacy:
All data stays on your computer:

• Portal credentials are stored locally in this app's SQLite database under your home directory — never sent to any server.
• Grade and homework data is stored in the same local database in your OS app-data folder.
• No data is sent to any server except TeacherEase itself (to check grades) and optionally your own SMTP server (if you enable email reports).
• No telemetry, no analytics, no tracking, no third-party services.
wizard.disclaimer.responsibleUse:
This app is designed to be a respectful, lightweight client:

• Only accesses data for children whose credentials you have provided.
• Does not enumerate other students, classes, or schools.
• Sends an identifiable User-Agent header so TeacherEase can contact the developer if needed.
• Fully open source — the complete source code is available for inspection.
about.title: About
gmail.heading: Gmail App Password setup
gmail.intro: Gmail requires an App Password for third-party SMTP clients like this one. It's a 16-character credential you generate once and paste into the Email settings. This guide walks through creating one.
gmail.why.title: Why you need this
gmail.why.body: As of May 2022, Google blocks plain-password SMTP authentication ("less secure app access"). App Passwords are the supported replacement — they act as limited-scope credentials that can be revoked independently of your main password.
gmail.before.title: Before you start
gmail.before.bullet1: You need 2-Step Verification turned on. App Passwords aren't available without it.
gmail.before.bullet2: Personal Gmail accounts work out of the box. Google Workspace (school/work) accounts may have App Passwords disabled by an admin — if so, ask your admin or use a personal account.
gmail.step1.title: Turn on 2-Step Verification
gmail.step1.body: Skip if you already have it on. Visit {link} and follow the prompts. You'll need a phone number to receive codes.
gmail.step2.title: Open the App Passwords page
gmail.step2.body: Visit {link}. If the page redirects to the 2-Step Verification setup, go back to Step 1 — App Passwords only appear after 2SV is active.
gmail.step3.title: Create a new app password
gmail.step3.body: Enter a name (e.g. {appName}) in the text box and click Create. Google shows the new password in a yellow box.
gmail.step4.title: Copy the 16-character password
gmail.step4.body1: Copy the 16-character string (spaces are optional — Gmail accepts either format). Paste it into this app at Settings → Email → Password, then click Save.
gmail.step4.body2: Important: Google only shows the password once. If you close the window without saving it, you'll need to revoke and re-create.
gmail.step5.title: Send a test email
gmail.step5.body: Back in Settings → Email, click Send test email. If you see "Test email sent" and the email arrives, you're done. Flip on the event toggles above to start receiving real notifications.
gmail.troubleshoot.title: Troubleshooting
gmail.troubleshoot.bullet1: "App passwords aren't available for your account" — your Workspace admin has disabled them. Use a personal Gmail account instead, or ask IT.
gmail.troubleshoot.bullet2: The apppasswords URL redirects back to 2SV setup — 2-Step Verification isn't fully enabled yet. Complete Step 1.
gmail.troubleshoot.bullet3: Test email fails with "535 5.7.8 Username and Password not accepted" — the 16-character password was mistyped or copied with a stray character. Re-generate a fresh one and paste carefully.
gmail.troubleshoot.bullet4: Lost the password — Google never shows it again. Revoke the old entry on the App Passwords page and create a new one.
gmail.docs.title: Official Google documentation
gmail.docs.body: For the canonical, always-current version of this guide (including any UI changes Google makes), see {link}.
notify.email.subject: TeacherEase Parent Companion: {heroLine}
notify.email.footer: TeacherEase Parent Companion · local notification · generated {time}
errors.scraper.login.noNetwork: Couldn't reach TeacherEase. Check your internet connection.
errors.scraper.login.badCredentials: Couldn't log in to TeacherEase. Double-check your email and password.
errors.scraper.homework.invalidUrl: That doesn't look like a valid URL. It should start with https://sites.google.com/
errors.scraper.homework.notGoogleSites: Homework URL must be a Google Sites page (starts with https://sites.google.com/).
errors.scraper.homework.unreachable: Couldn't reach that page. Check your internet connection and the URL.
errors.scraper.homework.unreachableHttp: Couldn't reach that page (HTTP {status}). Double-check the URL.
errors.scraper.homework.notGoogleSitesPa: Doesn't look like a Google Sites homework page. Double-check the URL.
